summary: Matched src/melee/gm/gmpause.c and updated shared scene/archive typing around SceneDesc and DynamicModelDesc so gmpause and several other scene-loading users access models, cameras, and fogs through typed descriptor fields instead of raw multi-pointer HSD_Joint/HSD_CObjDesc shapes. The PR also implemented gm_8016895C as a typed DynamicModelDesc animation helper and switched gmpause.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmpause.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmpause.c").

Reworked gmpause.c includes and local data: removed placeholder-style include usage, moved PauseData out of gm/types.h into gmpause.c, and made lbl_804D6700/lbl_804D6704 static rather than extern in the shown diff.

Updated gmpause archive loading from raw HSD_Joint pointer chains to SceneDesc: lbArchive_80016DBC("GmPause", &scene, "ScGamPause_scene_data", 0), HSD_JObjLoadJoint(scene->models[0]->joint), and gm_8016895C(jobj, scene->models[0], 0).
Adjusted pause analog stick animation math to use HSD_PadMasterStatus directly and deg_to_rad, with rotations applied to analog_stick for both X and Y in the excerpt.
Expanded SceneDesc in src/melee/sc/types.h so cameras are entries containing desc and anims, and fogs are entries containing desc and anims, instead of plain HSD_CObjDesc** and HSD_FogDesc**.
Implemented gm_8016895C(HSD_JObj*, DynamicModelDesc*, int idx) to null-check anims, matanims, and shapeanims arrays before passing selected entries to HSD_JObjAddAnimAll.
Updated gm_8016895C declaration in gm_1601.h to take DynamicModelDesc* and include sc/forward.h/sc/types.h where needed.
Removed a local gm_804D6840_t scene struct and local camera/fog wrapper structs from gm_1A4C.static.h, replacing them with SceneDesc* gm_804D6840 and DynamicModelDesc** gm_804D6844.

Converted multiple scene users to the refined SceneDesc layout: cameras[0].desc, cameras[0].anims[0], fogs[0].desc, fog animation access, models[i]->joint, and DynamicModelDesc* arguments to gm_8016895C.
Removed several casts to void** for lbArchive_80016DBC/lbArchive_LoadSymbols calls where typed output pointers now suffice.
Updated symbol sizes for lbl_803DA4B0 and lbl_803DA4D8 in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, shrinking the recorded string object sizes.
smart_moves: Used a shared SceneDesc refinement instead of keeping one-off local structs in gm_1A4C.c and vi0102.c, reducing duplicated guesses about archive scene layout.
Typed DynamicModelDesc deeply enough to make common model animation setup reusable through gm_8016895C, including anim, material anim, and shape anim selection.
Preserved nullable archive animation array behavior in gm_8016895C with ternary null checks before indexing, which is safer and likely closer to source intent than unconditional pointer chasing.
Moved PauseData out of the broad gm/types.h header and into gmpause.c, reducing unnecessary public type exposure.

Changed archive access from expressions like HSD_JObjLoadJoint(***joint), gm_804D6840->models[1][0], and gm_804D6844[i][0] to readable field-based access such as scene->models[0]->joint and gm_804D6844[i]->joint.
Updated dependent files in the same PR after changing SceneDesc, preventing the type tweak from leaving stale camera/model pointer assumptions elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: When archive symbol data appears as nested pointer chains, check whether it represents a common descriptor struct before preserving raw pointer syntax; typed SceneDesc/DynamicModelDesc access can unlock matching and improve readability.
A decomp match may require correcting shared headers and downstream users, not only editing the target C file.

For scene archives, cameras and fogs may be descriptor-plus-animation records rather than plain descriptor arrays; users should access cameras[slot].desc and cameras[slot].anims[index] when the refined SceneDesc type applies.
Model entries loaded from scene archives should often be treated as DynamicModelDesc* with a joint field and optional anim/matanim/shapeanim arrays.
If a helper like gm_8016895C is used across game mode and UI code, giving it the real descriptor type can simplify callsites and eliminate casts/double-dereference artifacts.
Local static structs that duplicate a likely project-wide archive format should be candidates for migration into shared headers once evidence appears in multiple files.
naming_conventions: SceneDesc is the shared name for a rendered scene/archive descriptor containing models, cameras, lights, and fogs.
DynamicModelDesc is the shared name for model descriptors passed to gm_8016895C and used for model joint plus animation data.
PauseData was kept as a local struct name in gmpause.c rather than a global gm/types.h type.
Function names remain address-derived where no better semantic name is known, e.g. gm_8016895C, fn_801A1134, fn_801A0E34.
Scene archive symbol strings use names like ScGamPause_scene_data, ScGamRegStaffroll_scene_data, ScNtcCommon_scene_data, and visual0102Scene.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked gmpause.c as Matching only after structural and callsite cleanup made it match.
Used PAD_STACK in at least gm_801AC6D8_OnEnter and existing stack padding in pause-related code to satisfy stack/layout-sensitive matching.
Replaced raw nested pointer dereferences with typed field access, which can change generated code shape while also documenting the real memory layout.
Removed unnecessary casts such as (void**) &typed_pointer in archive-loading calls to let typed lvalues drive matching and cleaner code generation.
Used deg_to_rad instead of a local literal/static float for degree-to-radian conversion in pause analog stick rotation code.
Adjusted config symbol sizes for two .data strings, likely to align symbol boundaries with the matched object/data layout.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt, and the single issue comment body was not included, so there is no evidence-grounded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further refine SceneDesc camera/fog animation element types, especially whether fog anims should be HSD_CameraAnim**, HSD_AObjDesc**, or another descriptor type.
Search for remaining HSD_Joint*** or HSD_Joint**** archive scene usages that could become DynamicModelDesc** or SceneDesc*.
Search for remaining raw cameras[0] calls on SceneDesc that should use cameras[0].desc after this layout change.
Check whether gm_8016895C eventually receives a semantic name describing model animation binding.
Look for other files still defining local scene archive structs duplicating SceneDesc.
